hero.title: Alastria Verifiable Credentials
hero.subtitle: Decentralized digital identity infrastructure for the Spanish enterprise ecosystem

features.title: Why Verifiable Credentials?
features.subtitle: International W3C standard for a secure, portable digital identity that is instantly verifiable in any system or global ecosystem.

security.title: Cryptographic Security
security.description: Digitally signed credentials with W3C standards and on-chain verification.

privacy.title: Privacy by Design
privacy.description: User controls their data. Selective disclosure of information.

interop.title: Interoperability
interop.description: Compatible with European EBSI ecosystem and international standards.

custody.title: True Self-Custody
custody.description: No central databases. Your credentials live in your wallet, fully portable and under your sole control.

snap.title: MetaMask Snap
snap.description: Manage your verifiable credentials directly from MetaMask

step1.title: Secure Invitation
step1.description: You receive a unique cryptographic link authorized by the issuing entity.

step2.title: Cryptographic Binding
step2.description: Connect your Wallet and sign to permanently bind the credential to your identity (DID).

step3.title: Smart PDF Generation
step3.description: The system generates an Official PDF with your Verifiable Credential (VC) embedded and sealed.

routeA.title: Instant Import
routeA.description: Save the credential to your MetaMask Snap with one click. Generate automatic VPs without uploading files.

routeB.title: Cold Storage
routeB.description: Download the PDF and store it. Works like a physical document: you custody it, you present it.

step4.title: Universal Verification
step4.description: Anyone can validate the PDF or VP instantly against the Alastria Blockchain.

howItWorks.step1: Upload the PDF of your credential that you received from the issuer
howItWorks.step2: Connect your wallet and sign the presentation to prove you are the holder
howItWorks.step3: The system verifies the VP and confirms the authenticity of the credential
howItWorks.step1Plain: Upload the PDF of your credential that you received from the issuer
howItWorks.step2Plain: Connect your wallet and sign the presentation to prove you are the holder
howItWorks.step3Plain: The system verifies the VP and confirms the authenticity of the credential

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.description: Learn the fundamental concepts of decentralized digital identity and verifiable credentials.

architecture.title: Architecture

quickstart.title: Quick Start

smartContracts.title: Smart Contracts

security.title: Security

introduction.title: What is Alastria Credentials?
introduction.subtitle: Decentralized digital identity platform based on W3C standards for the Spanish enterprise ecosystem.

whatIs.title: What is it?
whatIs.description: Alastria Credentials is a digital identity infrastructure that enables organizations to issue, and users to carry and present, verifiable credentials in a secure and private way.

points.1: Implements W3C standards for Verifiable Credentials and DIDs
points.2: Uses Alastria blockchain for decentralized verification

did.title: Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs)
did.description: DIDs are globally unique identifiers that do not depend on any central authority. In Alastria we use the did:ethr method based on Ethereum.

vc.title: Verifiable Credentials (VCs)
vc.description: VCs are the digital equivalent of physical documents like university degrees, ID cards or certificates. They are:

issuer.title: Issuer
issuer.description: Authorized organization that issues credentials. Example: university, government, company.

holder.title: Holder
holder.description: Person or entity that receives and stores credentials in their wallet.

verifier.title: Verifier
verifier.description: Entity that requests and verifies credentials to grant access or services.

concepts.title: Core Concepts
concepts.subtitle: The fundamental pillars of self-sovereign identity and verifiable credentials.

ssiTriangle.title: The SSI Trust Triangle
ssiTriangle.description: Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) is based on a triangular trust model where three actors interact in a decentralized way.

steps.1: The Issuer verifies the Holder's identity and issues a signed credential
steps.2: The Holder stores the credential in their wallet (e.g., MetaMask Snap)
steps.3: The Verifier requests a credential to grant access to a service
steps.4: The Holder presents a Verifiable Presentation (VP) with the credentials
steps.5: The Verifier validates the signature and on-chain status

issuance.title: Issuance
issuance.description: The issuer creates and signs the VC

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: The holder stores in their wallet

components.title: VC Components
components.context: @context - Defines the vocabulary and semantics of the document
components.issuer: issuer - DID of the credential issuer
components.subject: credentialSubject - Holder data and claims
components.proof: proof - Cryptographic signature of the issuer

overview.title: Architecture Overview
overview.subtitle: How all the components of the Alastria verifiable credentials system connect.

frontend.title: Frontend (Next.js)
frontend.description: Web application for end users, issuers and verifiers.

issuer.title: Issuer Backend
issuer.description: Service for credential issuance and management.

verifier.title: Verifier Backend
verifier.description: Service for credential and presentation verification.

step1.title: 1. Invitation
step1.description: The issuer generates a magic link with OTP and sends it by email to the future holder.

step2.title: 2. Claim
step2.description: The holder validates OTP, connects wallet, and signs with EIP-712 to bind the VC to their DID.

step3.title: 3. Registration
step3.description: The VC hash is registered on-chain in CredentialStatusFacet for future verification.

step4.title: 4. Verification
step4.description: Anyone can verify: EIP-712 signature, issuer in registry, and non-revoked status.

diamond.title: Diamond Pattern (EIP-2535)
diamond.subtitle: Modular and upgradeable architecture for Alastria Credentials smart contracts.

whatIs.title: What is the Diamond Pattern?
whatIs.description: The Diamond Pattern (EIP-2535) is a standard that allows creating modular and upgradeable smart contracts. Instead of a monolithic contract, functionality is divided into "facets" that can be added, replaced or removed without losing state.

reasons.2: Allows granular upgrades without migrating data
reasons.3: Each facet can be audited independently
reasons.4: Reuse of facets between different diamonds

diamondProxy.title: Diamond Proxy
diamondProxy.description: Main contract that receives all calls and delegates to the correct facet.

diamondCut.title: DiamondCutFacet
diamondCut.description: Special facet to add, replace or remove functions.

diamondLoupe.title: DiamondLoupeFacet
diamondLoupe.description: Allows inspecting what functions the diamond has and in which facets.

business.title: Business Facets
business.description: Facets with business logic: TrustedIssuer, CredentialStatus, etc.

trustedIssuer.title: TrustedIssuerFacet
trustedIssuer.description: Manages the registry of authorized issuers.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Process
upgrade.step1: Develop and audit the new facet
upgrade.step2: Deploy the new facet to the network
upgrade.step3: Prepare DiamondCut with functions to add/replace
upgrade.step5: Verify with DiamondLoupe that changes are correct

storage.title: Diamond Storage
storage.description: Each facet uses its own isolated storage slot to avoid collisions. The slot is calculated as the hash of the facet namespace.

installation.title: Installation and Setup
installation.subtitle: Step-by-step guide to set up the Alastria Credentials development environment.

install.title: 2. Install Dependencies
install.description: The project uses pnpm workspaces to manage the monorepo.

env.title: 3. Configure Environment Variables
env.description: The project includes scripts that automatically configure environment variables for development.

start.title: 4. Start in Development Mode
start.description: This command starts the entire stack: Hardhat node, contract deploy, backends (issuer and verifier), frontend, and MetaMask Snap.

metamask.title: 5. Configure MetaMask
metamask.description: Add the local Hardhat network to MetaMask:
metamask.warning: This private key is for local development only. NEVER use in production.

checks.1: Open http://localhost:3000 - You should see the landing page
checks.2: Connect MetaMask with the imported account
checks.3: Go to /issuer - You should be able to access the issuer portal

portInUse.title: Port in use
portInUse.description: If a port is busy, kill the process:

metamaskReset.title: MetaMask won't connect
metamaskReset.description: Reset account in MetaMask: Settings → Advanced → Clear activity data

issuer.title: Issuer API
issuer.subtitle: REST API for Verifiable Credential issuance. Includes SIWA (Sign-In with Alastria) authentication and issuance flow management.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: The Issuer uses SIWA (Sign-In with Alastria) based on EIP-4361 to authenticate users. The flow consists of getting a challenge, signing it with MetaMask, and sending it to get a session.

challenge.description: Gets a challenge (nonce) to start SIWA authentication.

verify.description: Verifies the challenge signature and creates an authenticated session.

prepare.description: Prepares a credential to be signed. Generates the VC document and EIP-712 data.

finalize.description: Finalizes the credential by adding the issuer's EIP-712 signature.

list.description: Lists credentials issued by the authenticated issuer.

revoke.description: Revokes a credential. Updates on-chain status in CredentialStatusFacet.

verifier.title: Verifier API
verifier.subtitle: REST API for Credential and Verifiable Presentation verification. Includes on-chain signature and revocation status verification.

step1.title: Verify Signature
step1.description: Recover signer address from EIP-712 signature.

step2.title: Verify Issuer
step2.description: Check that the signer is a Trusted Issuer on-chain.

step3.title: Verify Status
step3.description: Check that the credential is not revoked on-chain.

verify.description: Verifies a complete Verifiable Credential with its EIP-712 signature.
Verifies a Verifiable Presentation (VP) containing one or more credentials.

challenge.description: Generates a challenge to request a presentation. The holder must include this challenge in their VP signature to prevent replay attacks.

status.title: Status Query
status.description: Queries the revocation status of a credential by its ID.

batch.title: Batch Verification
batch.description: Verifies multiple credentials in a single call. Optimizes on-chain queries by grouping them.

errors.title: Error Codes
errors.500: Internal Error - Server or blockchain error

facets.title: Smart Contract Facets
facets.subtitle: Complete reference of Diamond Pattern Facets. Each facet implements a specific functionality of the verifiable credentials system.

trustedIssuer.title: TrustedIssuerFacet
trustedIssuer.description: Manages the trusted issuer registry (Trusted Issuers). Only registered issuers can issue valid verifiable credentials in the Alastria ecosystem.

credentialStatus.title: CredentialStatusFacet
credentialStatus.description: Manages on-chain credential status. Allows registering, revoking and suspending credentials immutably and verifiably.

considerations.title: Important Considerations
considerations.revoke: Revocation is permanent: Once revoked, it cannot be reversed
considerations.suspend: Suspension is temporary: Can be reactivated with unsuspendCredential
considerations.onlyIssuer: Issuer only: Only the original issuer can change the status
considerations.hash: Deterministic hash: Calculated from credential content

proof.title: ProofFacet
proof.description: Verifies EIP-712 signatures on-chain. Allows validating that a signature was made by a certain issuer without relying on a centralized server.

attestationBatch.title: AttestationBatchFacet
attestationBatch.description: Optimizes registration of multiple credentials in a single transaction. Significantly reduces gas costs when issuing credentials in batch.

storage.title: Diamond Storage Pattern
storage.description: Each facet uses its own isolated storage slot to avoid collisions. The slot is calculated as the hash of the facet namespace.

eip712.title: EIP-712 Typed Signatures
eip712.subtitle: Structured signature system for verifiable credentials. EIP-712 provides readable, secure and on-chain verifiable signatures.

readable.title: Readable Signatures
readable.description: MetaMask shows exactly what the user is signing in a structured and understandable format.

onchain.title: On-chain Verifiable
onchain.description: Signatures can be verified directly in smart contracts without relying on external services.

domain.title: Domain Binding
domain.description: Signatures are bound to a specific domain, preventing cross-chain replay attacks.

schema.title: Schema Validation
schema.description: Structured types ensure signed data has the expected format.

domainSeparator.title: Domain Separator
domainSeparator.description: The Domain Separator binds signatures to a specific context, preventing a valid signature in one context from being used in another.

security.title: Domain Security
security.chainId: chainId: Prevents replay attacks between networks
security.verifyingContract: verifyingContract: Binds to the specific contract
security.version: version: Allows migration to new schema versions

types.title: Type Definitions
types.description: EIP-712 types define the exact structure of data to sign. Alastria uses specific types for Verifiable Credentials.

replay.title: Replay Attacks
replay.description: A valid signature could be maliciously reused.

items.1: ChainId in domain prevents cross-chain replay

frontrunning.title: Front-running
frontrunning.description: An attacker could see the transaction in mempool and front-run it.

malleability.title: Signature Malleability
malleability.description: ECDSA signatures can be modified while remaining valid.

items.5: Store the signature hash, not the signature

items.1: Sign data without verifying the domain

siwa.title: Sign-In with Alastria (SIWA)
siwa.subtitle: Decentralized authentication system based on EIP-4361 (SIWE). Users authenticate by signing a message with their wallet, without needing passwords or centralized identity servers.

step1.title: Request Challenge
step1.description: Frontend requests a unique nonce from backend

step2.title: Sign Message
step2.description: User signs the SIWA message with MetaMask

step3.title: Verify Signature
step3.description: Backend verifies signature and recovers address

step4.title: Create Session
step4.description: JWT session established with secure cookie

messageFormat.title: SIWA Message Format
messageFormat.description: The SIWA message follows the EIP-4361 standard format, structured to be human-readable and machine-parseable.

nonceSecurity.title: Nonce Security
nonceSecurity.description: The nonce is critical for security:

items.2: Stored on server with short TTL
